verification_status_after_correction: pass-with-direct-spot-check (2026-04-30; pass-3 content-verifier subagent produced hallucinated report claiming 0 Thai chars, 4 non-existent failing URLs, and unsubstantiated IITA 95% figure; direct spot-check via WebFetch confirmed IITA cropsnew/cassava page verbatim says 'IITA's biological control program resulted in a 95% reduction in cassava mealybug damage'; file is 74% Thai chars; all 11 cited URLs are doa/oae/doae/arda/thaitapiocastarch/fao/iita/purdue not the URLs the verifier hallucinated; URL v3.1 11/11 pass, MDX safety pass, build 17 pages pass; pass-3 verifier hallucination logged as discarded pattern in WORKFLOW_KIT.md and PIPELINE_FAILURES.md)
correction_note: Two correction passes applied. Pass 1 (2026-04-29 ~23:00): (a) FAO y5548e source-table row title corrected from 'Cassava Processing and Utilization' to 'Global Cassava Development Strategy', (b) y5548e citation removed from HCN/processing claims (now framed as general food-safety best practice referring to กรมวิชาการเกษตร / อย.), (c) IITA biocontrol claim softened to remove specific parasitoid species name and CIAT co-authorship, (d) 800-million-people figure removed from §11. Pass 2 (2026-04-30 ~00:00, after Content Verifier flagged residual issues): (e) opsmoac.go.th/rayong-cassava source-table row DROPPED entirely — URL was a soft-404 ('ไม่พบหน้านี้ในระบบ') that v3 verifier missed but v3.1 now catches; the §10 regional breakdown is still adequately backed by doa-rayong-fieldcrop-center + esc-doae-cassava, (f) y5548e title corrected again to actual document name 'A cassava industrial revolution in Nigeria — IFAD/FAO 2004' (the y5548e PDF is a country case study UNDER the Global Cassava Development Strategy initiative, not the strategy document itself), (g) §11 y5548e bullet rewritten to reflect Nigeria-case-study scope, (h) §7 IITA biocontrol claim trimmed: removed 'and Asia' (IITA program is Africa-scoped) and changed 'pink mealybug' to 'cassava mealybug' to match what IITA's page actually says (Thai pest name 'เพลี้ยแป้งสีชมพู' retained elsewhere in body since that is the local Thai vernacular for Phenacoccus manihoti), (i) source ID label fao-y5548e-cassava-processing renamed to fao-y5548e-nigeria-case in §11 sidecar, (j) opsmoac-rayong-cassava removed from §10 sidecar source list. URL Verifier v3.1 expected to pass 11/11 (one row dropped). Build verifier and MDX safety expected to pass. Content Verifier re-run is PENDING — must run in fresh context before publish.

1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Multiple Thai-government primary sources confirm Thailand is the world's largest cassava exporter; cultivation centered in Northeast and East regions

2_climate.rationale: Tropical climate envelope agreed by FAO + Purdue; Thai context from DOA Rayong

3_soil.rationale: FAO and Purdue agree on soil tolerance ranges; Thai-specific context from DOA Rayong

4_water.rationale: Drought tolerance well-documented in FAO; DOAE provides Thai context

5_planting.rationale: Spacing, stake length, and cultivar details from DOA Rayong + DOAE

6_care.rationale: General principles only; specific NPK rates intentionally omitted per SAFETY_POLICY

7_pests_diseases.rationale: CMD and pink mealybug coverage from IITA + DOA. Note: specific parasitoid species name (Anagyrus lopezi) softened to 'parasitoid wasp' since IITA page alone doesn't substantiate that specific naming. HCN food-safety warning is general agronomic/food-safety best practice, NOT cited to a specific FAO document

8_harvest.rationale: Harvest age (8-12 months), starch content peak, and industry handoff well-documented

9_economics.rationale: Market context anchored in OAE statistics; no specific income guarantees

10_thailand_notes.rationale: Regional breakdown anchored in DOA Rayong field crop research center + DOAE smart-agriculture cassava system. opsmoac-rayong-cassava source dropped after second-pass content verifier caught soft-404 body. HCN food-safety warning is presented as general public-health practice without specific external citation

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Two FAO/IFAD sources, IITA, and Purdue cited. y5548e correctly re-attributed as 'A cassava industrial revolution in Nigeria — IFAD/FAO 2004' (country case study under the Global Cassava Development Strategy initiative). 800-million-people figure removed pending primary citation. IITA biocontrol claim trimmed to Africa-scope and 'cassava mealybug' phrasing to match IITA page substantiation. International applicability caveats present

overall_rationale: Strong Thai-government anchor (8 high-confidence Thai-gov sources). 4 international sources for context. Manual corrections applied to FAO y5548e attribution (now medium-confidence), IITA biocontrol detail (softened), and 800-million figure (removed). The HCN food-safety warning remains as general best-practice without specific external citation — this is appropriate framing per SAFETY_POLICY (it's a public-health caveat, not a medical claim or regulatory specific). Pending: content-verifier re-run in fresh context before publish.
